page.content: The data presented in the table offers valuable insights into the relationship between various genotypes and their corresponding SC classifications and PTG scores. The SC index categorizes the genotypes based on their suitability for specific applications, with classifications ranging from fully sustainable to less favorable.

lines.text: The data presented in the table offers valuable insights into the relationship between various genotypes and their corresponding
SC classifications and PTG scores. The SC index categorizes the genotypes based on their suitability for specific applications,
with classifications ranging from fully sustainable to less favorable.

page.content:
Notably, the column labeled "PTG score" provides a quantitative measure of the performance of each genotype, facilitating comparisons across different entries. 

For instance, the genotypes P-7-68 and P-8-14 demonstrate significant differences in their respective SC classifications, which can inform selection criteria for future research and application. Additionally, the "Intake" column reflects the average oil/spread intake, measured at 41 ± 8 g/day and 39 ± 7 g/day for the groups represented by N = 19 and N = 18, respectively. This metric is crucial for understanding the practical implications of each genotype's performance in real-world scenarios. Such data not only enhances our understanding of genotypic variations but also aids in optimizing selection strategies for improved outcomes in related agricultural practices. Overall, the table serves as a foundational reference for ongoing studies in this domain.
